questions.question: Define a stack and explain its operations (push, pop, peek, isEmpty, isFull) with an array-based implementation in C.
keyPoints: Definition of stack as LIFO data structure
Stack structure with array and top variable
Push: check overflow (top == MAX-1), increment top, store element
Pop: check underflow (top == -1), read element, decrement top
Complete C code with main function demonstrating operations

questions.question: Explain the various applications of stacks with suitable examples.
keyPoints: Brief explanation of each with examples

questions.question: Write a C program to check whether a given expression has balanced parentheses using a stack.
keyPoints: Pop and match for closing brackets
Check if stack is empty at the end

questions.question: Compare and contrast stack and queue data structures.
keyPoints: Application differences (DFS vs BFS, undo vs scheduling)

questions.question: What are the conditions for stack overflow and stack underflow? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: Step-by-step trace showing push operations filling the stack
Demonstrating overflow when stack is full

questions.question: List the key operations supported by a stack and explain their time complexity.
keyPoints: Push, pop, peek, isEmpty, isFull operations
Time complexity of each operation (O(1) for push, pop, peek)

questions.question: Explain the concept of backtracking and how stacks are used in backtracking algorithms.
keyPoints: Use of stacks to store and retrieve states
Example of a backtracking algorithm (e.g., maze solving)
